title: Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite (Cross-Sectional Standardisation)
summary: Normalise each signal dimension score to a Z-score before compositing, so that dimensions with naturally wider numeric ranges don't structurally dominate the 9-score average. The multi-factor ETH trading research (ACM 2025) found optimal Z-score thresholds of ±1.0 for buy/sell signals. Cross-sectional standardisation is a standard quant technique validated across hundreds of studies — it ensures the composite reflects genuine signal quality rather than raw scale differences between dimensions.
expected_impact: +1-2% improvement in signal consistency; eliminates dimension-scale bias where larger raw scores from some dimensions dominate composite unfairly
